eval_prompt: Run a temporary batch data migration job on Aether cluster (python:3.11, python migrate.py, /data volume), check logs, then clean up

criteria.description: Must generate and submit a batch-type Nomad job (not service)
criteria.notes: Job spec clearly shows Type: batch. Submitted via Nomad HTTP API with EvalID returned.

criteria.description: Must complete run -> logs -> clean three-step flow
criteria.notes: All three steps documented: run (job submission), logs (stdout/stderr retrieved), clean (purged dev-temp-* jobs). Skill Flow Adherence table confirms all subcommands executed.
image: python:3.11 in job spec JSON.

criteria.description: Must check logs after execution to confirm results
criteria.notes: Retrieved stdout (empty) and stderr (migrate.py not found). Provided clear analysis: failure is expected since no script was deployed. Migration Result: FAILED.

criteria.description: Must generate and submit a batch-type Nomad job (not service)
criteria.notes: Job type is batch. Submitted via Nomad HTTP API.

criteria.description: Must complete run -> logs -> clean three-step flow
criteria.notes: Run (sections 2-4), logs (section 4 stdout/stderr/events), clean (section 6 with verification).

criteria.description: Must check logs after execution to confirm results
criteria.notes: Full stdout logs captured showing diagnostic output. Stderr confirmed empty. Task events listed. Analysis provided in section 5.

output_a.content_notes: Full JSON job spec shown. Clear problem-solving narrative (host volume -> bind mount fallback). Transparent about failure with practical recommendations. Issues table and Skill Flow Adherence table add clarity.
output_a.structure_notes: Clean three-step structure matching skill subcommands. Tables for issues and adherence. Code block for job spec. Clear section separators.

output_b.content_notes: Includes cluster connectivity verification. Proactively created /data directory and resubmitted. Modified command to diagnostic bash script (not literal python migrate.py). More thorough cleanup (directory, local file, job). Creative but deviates from literal request.
output_b.structure_notes: Seven well-numbered sections covering full lifecycle. Multiple tables (nodes, events, cleanup). Polished presentation with clear headers and logical flow.

blind_comparison.rationale: Both outputs pass all criteria. Output A wins because it more faithfully follows the skill's documented run->logs->clean subcommand pattern and is transparent about the actual failure (migrate.py not found). Output B achieved exit code 0 by substituting a diagnostic shell script for the requested 'python migrate.py' command -- clever but a deviation from the literal request. Output A's structured skill adherence tracking demonstrates the value of the skill framework. Output B has slightly better structure (score 5 vs 4) and more thorough cleanup, but the command substitution is a notable concern for faithfulness to user intent.
